# byplay usage guide for AI agents

`byplay` is an in-browser HLS/M3U8 diagnostic player: paste a stream URL, tune
hls.js ABR / buffer / retry / ad-filter knobs live, and watch a real-time stats
+ event-log panel. It is a **fully static Next.js export** (`output: 'export'`)
— no backend, no API routes, no worker, no database, no env/secrets, no
Cloudflare bindings. All playback, ad filtering, and telemetry are client-side.

Primary docs:

- README: ./README.md
- Design spec (authoritative): ./DESIGN.md

Part of the `@cdlab/projects-monorepo`; run all commands from the repo root with
`pnpm --filter @cdlab/byplay <script>`.

## What it is

- **One screen, one hook.** `src/app/[locale]/page.tsx` (`HlsPage`) holds `url`
  / `HlsConfig` / `playbackRate` and wires config cards to `useHlsPlayer`.
- **The core is `src/hooks/use-hls-player.ts`** — owns `HlsConfig`,
  `DEFAULT_HLS_CONFIG`, driver selection, the retry loop, the ad-filter loader,
  and a 200-entry newest-first log ring.
- **Static export.** No server runtime. `src/middleware.ts` (next-intl locale
  middleware) does NOT execute; locale routing is static + `/` → `/en` redirect
  (`src/app/page.tsx`). Do not describe an edge/SSR runtime.

## Playback flow (loadSource in use-hls-player.ts)

`loadSource(url, config)` destroys any prior `Hls`, clears native `src`, then
picks ONE driver:

1. `/\.(mp4|webm|ogg|ogv|mov|avi|mkv)(\?.*)?$/i` matches → native `<video src>`
   (direct file). Sets `isDirectVideo`, which hides all HLS-only config cards.
2. else `Hls.isSupported()` → `new Hls(mergedConfig)` + `loadSource` +
   `attachMedia` (hls.js MSE path).
3. else `canPlayType('application/vnd.apple.mpegurl')` → native `<video src>`
   (Safari HLS).
4. else → `state.error = 'HLS is not supported in this browser'`.

Key behaviors:

- **Autoplay muted-fallback**: `play()` unmuted first, on reject set `muted` and
  retry (browser autoplay policy).
- **Two retry layers**: hls.js `*LoadingMaxRetry` config AND an app-level loop —
  fatal `NETWORK_ERROR` → `hls.startLoad()` (≤3), fatal `MEDIA_ERROR` →
  `hls.recoverMediaError()` (≤3), then `hls.destroy()`. Non-fatal errors are
  logged only.
- **HEVC/H.265** sets a SOFT warning on `MANIFEST_PARSED` but still plays.
- hls.js config merges user knobs with fixed constants (`abrEwmaFast/Slow=3/9`,
  `*MaxRetryTimeout=64000`, `*RetryDelay=1000`) — these are not UI-exposed.

## Config model (HlsConfig)

`HlsConfig` = a `Pick` of hls.js `HlsConfig` (18 fields: enableWorker,
lowLatencyMode, maxBufferLength/maxMaxBufferLength/maxBufferSize/maxBufferHole/
backBufferLength, startFragPrefetch, abrEwmaDefaultEstimate/abrBandWidthFactor/
abrBandWidthUpFactor, frag/manifest/levelLoadingMaxRetry, fragLoadingRetryDelay,
frag/manifest/levelLoadingTimeOut) + app-level `autoPlay`, `adFilterMode`,
`adKeywords`. Defaults in `DEFAULT_HLS_CONFIG`.

- Config knobs take effect only on the NEXT Load (baked into the `Hls`
  constructor). Playback rate and manual level apply live via `setPlaybackRate`
  / `setLevel` (NOT part of `HlsConfig`).
- Each card under `src/components/player/` is controlled via
  `onUpdateConfig(key, value)`; barrel `index.ts` exports them.

## Ad filter (src/lib/m3u8-utils.ts + m3u8-ad-detector.ts)

- Active only when `adFilterMode !== 'off'`. Injected as a subclass of
  `Hls.DefaultConfig.loader` that rewrites `manifest`/`level` responses with
  `filterM3u8Ad` before hls.js parses them — per request, in the browser.
- `filterM3u8Ad(content, baseUrl, mode, keywords, stats)` — 5 stages: heuristic
  block scoring → SCTE-35 CUE strip → keyword backtracking → aggressive
  discontinuity strip → relative→absolute URL normalization.
- Thresholds: `heuristic` ≥ 5.0, `aggressive` ≥ 3.0. Modes: off/keyword/
  heuristic/aggressive.
- `m3u8-ad-detector.ts`: `parseBlocks`, `learnMainPattern`, `scoreBlock`,
  `shouldFilterBlock`, `AD_PATH_KEYWORDS`. Each run logs a `FilterStats` summary.

## Cross-tool integration

`source-card.tsx` renders a link to `https://vidl.pages.dev/<locale>?url=<url>`
(jump-to-vidl to download the current stream). Locale is preserved.

## Data model / storage

NONE. No database, no localStorage, no persistence. `HlsPlayerState`,
`HlsLogEntry` (200-cap ring), and `FilterStats` are the only "schemas" — all
ephemeral React state, reset on reload.

## Config / bindings

- No env vars, no secrets, no Cloudflare bindings. Only build-time input is
  `BUILD_TIME` injected in `next.config.ts`; the sole `process.env` read in
  `src` is `process.env.BUILD_TIME` (footer stamp).
- Deep link: `?url=` / `?source=` auto-loads on mount if the URL ends in a media
  extension (`.m3u8/.m3u/.mp4/.webm/.ogg/.ts/.mpd`).
- i18n: two locales `['en','zh']` (`src/i18n/routing.ts`), messages typed via
  next-intl (`messages/en.d.json.ts` generated).

## Gotchas for agents

- The README's "playback events are reported to the byplay-log worker" is
  ASPIRATIONAL — NOT implemented. There is no `fetch`/`sendBeacon`/endpoint or
  any `byplay-log` reference in `src`. Do not document it as a feature.
- `public/vplayer.js` + `src/types/vplayer.d.ts` (createVzanPlayer/window.vplayer)
  are DEAD leftover from a prior player; nothing in `src` uses them. Some
  `messages/*` keys also date from it.
- `@cdlab/driftflake` is declared in `package.json` but never imported in `src`.
- `output: 'export'` means no server: middleware is inert, no SSR/edge, no env.
- HLS-only cards hide when `isDirectVideo` — a direct file skips hls.js entirely.
- Config changes need a re-Load to apply; rate/level apply live.
- No tests exist (no runner, no test files).

## Common commands

```bash
pnpm --filter @cdlab/byplay dev        # http://byplay.localhost:3355
pnpm --filter @cdlab/byplay lint       # next lint
pnpm --filter @cdlab/byplay typecheck  # tsc --noEmit
pnpm --filter @cdlab/byplay build      # next build → static out/
pnpm --filter @cdlab/byplay build:cf   # @cloudflare/next-on-pages
```
